title: Mark/index dislocation alert
hypothesis: When a high-liquidity perpetual future's mark price diverges from its index price beyond a rolling threshold, the window may identify liquidation stress, exchange-specific pricing risk or a later compression alert. The first implementation should only filter and annotate existing candidates rather than trigger standalone entries.

inputs: mark and index price series aligned to 1m or 5m bars
perp candles or trade-derived bars for forward return labeling
funding rates near the dislocation window
optional base strategy signal to be filtered
entry_rules: abs(dislocation_zscore) exceeds configured threshold over the lookback window
mark and index observations are fresh and aligned
perp trade-derived bars confirm the window is not a single bad print
signal is used to warn, block or down-rank existing candidates by default
standalone entry remains disabled until replay proves cost-adjusted benefit
exit_rules: mark or index feed becomes stale
exchange reports abnormal market or instrument status
manual review rejects the venue or symbol for this window

test_plan: Start with Binance BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT and SOLUSDT USDT perpetuals using 1m mark and index price windows.
Compute dislocation_bps and rolling z-score over 1d, 3d and 7d lookbacks.
Compare forward 5m, 15m and 1h returns after expansion, compression and neutral windows.
Use the signal as a filter for funding_carry_vol_filter, oi_confirmed_momentum and perp_premium_zscore_mean_reversion candidates.
Record false-positive suppression cost and false-negative stress misses separately.
Do not enable standalone execution until replay shows stable cost-adjusted improvement across symbols and months.
risk_controls: Default action is alert or down-rank, not trade entry.
Filter out stale mark/index data and single bad-print windows.
Do not compare mark/index values across exchanges until each venue's calculation and index constituents are documented.
Disable the signal during exchange maintenance, abnormal instrument status or manual data-quality uncertainty.
Keep all outputs research-only; no production config, live trading or auto-deployment.
